Article.ArticleTitle: Fast Prototyping Ceramic Gas Flow Sensors for Harsh Operating Conditions.

Abstract.AbstractText: The technology development for the mass ceramic gas flow sensor (CGFS) adopted for harsh operating conditions is presented. The main characteristic of this technology is its simplicity and affordability for mass fast prototyping of CGFS with a limited set of technological equipment. Special attention is paid to the discussion of the technological and operational materials' compatibility, flexibility, and speed of their processing to adapt the best mass flow sensor design option. The CGFS, designed and manufactured in just a few days, was tested in conditions close to the real ones and demonstrated the ability to measure gas flow in the range from 0.21 m/s to 1.25 m/s, with a constant power consumption of 152 mW@346 °C.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare no conflicts of interest. Andrey Kasenko, Pavel Shchur, Ekaterina Anatolevna Drach, Ivan Borzunov, Vasily V. Egorov, and Boris Prudnikov are employees of INTER Limited Liability Company. The paper reflects the views of the scientists, and not the company.
